data.content:
### Playwright: API Monitoring Example

Source: https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/playwright-monitor

This Playwright test monitors an API endpoint by making an HTTP GET request and verifying the response. It uses `fetch` to call a coffee API, checks if the response is successful, and then filters the data to find items exceeding a maximum price. If expensive items are found, it logs a warning and throws an error, facilitating API health checks and alerting. It also shows how to use environment variables for authentication tokens.

```javascript
[label API monitoring example]
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';

test('No expensive coffee in API', async () => {
  const URL = 'https://api.sampleapis.com/coffee/hot';
  const MAX_PRICE = 2.9;

  const response = await fetch(URL, {
    method: 'GET',
    headers: {
      'Authorization': `Bearer ${process.env.API_TOKEN}`,
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    },
  });
  console.debug("API Response:", response);
  expect(response.ok).toBeTruthy();

  const data = await response.json();
  console.debug("API JSON data:", data);

  const expensiveItems = data
    .filter(item => item.price > MAX_PRICE);

  if (expensiveItems.length > 0) {
    console.warn('🚨 Found expensive items:');
    expensiveItems.forEach(item => {
      console.warn(`- ${item.title}: $${item.price}`);
    });

    throw new Error(`Found ${expensiveItems.length} item(s) over $${MAX_PRICE}.`);
  }
});
```

--------------------------------

### List Status Page Resources cURL Example

Source: https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/api/list-existing-resources-of-a-status-page

This example demonstrates how to list status page resources using cURL. It requires your status page ID and an API token for authentication. The endpoint supports GET requests and includes pagination.

```shell
curl --request GET \
  --url https://uptime.betterstack.com/api/v2/status-pages/123456789/resources \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright: Test User Signup on Mobile

Source: https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/playwright-monitor

This Playwright test verifies that users can sign up on a website from a mobile viewport. It sets the viewport size, navigates to the specified URL, and checks for the presence of the 'Sign up' text on the page. This scenario helps ensure critical user flows are functional on mobile devices.

```javascript
[label Test that users can sign up on mobile]
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';

test('mobile page contains Sign up', async ({ page }) => {
  await page.setViewportSize({ width: 360, height: 640 }); 
  await page.goto('https://betterstack.com/');
  const pageContent = await page.textContent('body');
  expect(pageContent).toContain('Sign up');
}); 
```

--------------------------------

### List Catalog Attributes using cURL

Source: https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/api/list-all-catalog-attributes

Example of how to list catalog attributes using cURL. This command sends a GET request to the specified URL with the necessary authorization header. Ensure you replace `$TOKEN` with your actual API token.

```shell
curl --request GET \
  --url https://uptime.betterstack.com/api/v2/catalog/relations/37/attributes \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright: Test User Sign-in with Environment Variables

Source: https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/playwright-monitor

This Playwright test demonstrates how to securely handle sensitive information, such as passwords, using environment variables. It navigates to a login page, fills in the email and password fields (retrieving the password from `process.env.PASSWORD`), clicks the sign-in button, and waits for the dashboard URL to confirm successful login. This is essential for security in automated testing.

```javascript
[label Test that users can sign in]
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';

test('users can sign in', async ({ page }) => {
  await page.goto('https://app.example.com/');
  
  await page.getByLabel('E-mail').fill('user@example.com');
  await page.getByLabel('Password').fill(process.env.PASSWORD);
  await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Sign in' }).click();
  
  await page.waitForURL('https://app.example.com/dashboard');
});
```

--------------------------------

### List Email Integrations (cURL)

Source: https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/api/list-all-email-integrations

This example demonstrates how to fetch a list of all email integrations using cURL. It requires an API token for authentication. The request specifies the GET method and the relevant API endpoint.

```shell
curl --request GET \
  --url https://uptime.betterstack.com/api/v2/email-integrations \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"
```

--------------------------------

### Example Runbook Instructions with TODO List (Markdown)

Source: https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/runbooks

This markdown snippet demonstrates how to structure runbook instructions, including a title, scenario description, and an interactive to-do list for incident response steps. It utilizes markdown formatting for clarity and includes example commands.

```markdown
[label Example runbook instructions with TODO list]
## When to Use
Triggered when CPU > 90% for 5+ minutes on a web server.

## Steps
- [ ] **Acknowledge the Alert**
- [ ] **Find the Affected Server**
   - Use logs or metrics dashboard to identify the instance/container
   - Example: `aws ecs list-tasks --cluster web-prod`
- [ ] **SSH or Access Container**
   - `ssh ec2-user@<instance-ip>`
- [ ] **Diagnose the Issue**
   - Run `top` or `htop` to find CPU-heavy process
   - Check application logs
- [ ] **Fix or Mitigate**
   - Restart service if needed
   - Scale up if traffic is legitimate
- [ ] **Verify**
   - CPU drops below 70%
   - No 5xx errors
   - App is responsive

```

--------------------------------

### SSH Command Example for Server Access

Source: https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/runbooks

This code snippet provides an example of using SSH to connect to an EC2 instance. It specifies the username and a placeholder for the instance's IP address, useful for direct server access during incident response.

```bash
ssh ec2-user@<instance-ip>
```

--------------------------------

### Get On-Call Schedule cURL Example

Source: https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/api/get-a-single-on-call-calendar

This example demonstrates how to retrieve a specific on-call schedule using cURL. It specifies the HTTP method, URL, and the required Authorization header. The `schedule_id` can be a specific ID or 'default'.

```shell
curl --request GET \
  --url "https://uptime.betterstack.com/api/v2/on-calls/default" \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"
```

--------------------------------

### Get Splunk Integration (cURL)

Source: https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/api/single-splunk-on-call-integration

This example demonstrates how to retrieve a Splunk On-Call integration using cURL. It requires the integration ID and an authorization token. The request sends a GET request to the specified API endpoint.

```shell
curl --request GET \
  --url https://uptime.betterstack.com/api/v2/splunk-on-call/17 \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"
```

--------------------------------

### cURL Example: List Monitors with Pagination

Source: https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/api/pagination

This example demonstrates how to use cURL to fetch a paginated list of monitors from the Better Stack Uptime API. It specifies 20 monitors per page and requests the 3rd page. Ensure you replace '$TOKEN' with your actual API token.

```shell
curl --request GET \
  --url "https://uptime.betterstack.com/api/v2/monitors?per_page=20&page=3" \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"
```

--------------------------------

### List Datadog Integrations - cURL Example

Source: https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/api/list-all-datadog-integrations

This cURL command demonstrates how to fetch a list of all Datadog integrations. It requires an Authorization header with a bearer token. The response includes integration details and pagination information.

```shell
curl --request GET \
  --url https://uptime.betterstack.com/api/v2/datadog-integrations \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"
```

--------------------------------

### Get Single Heartbeat

Source: https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/api/get-a-single-hearbeat

Retrieves details of a specific heartbeat using its ID. Includes path parameters, headers, and example responses.

```APIDOC
## GET /api/v2/heartbeats/{heartbeat_id}

### Description
Returns a single heartbeat by ID.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
https://uptime.betterstack.com/api/v2/heartbeats/{heartbeat_id}

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **heartbeat_id** (string) - Required - The ID of your requested heartbeat

#### Headers
- **Authorization** (string) - Required - Bearer `$TOKEN`

### Request Example
```shell
curl --request GET \
  --url https://uptime.betterstack.com/api/v2/heartbeats/12345 \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN"
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **data** (object) - Contains the heartbeat details.
  - **id** (string) - The unique identifier for the heartbeat.
  - **type** (string) - The type of the resource, typically "heartbeat".
  - **attributes** (object) - Contains the heartbeat's attributes.
    - **url** (string) - The URL being monitored.
    - **name** (string) - The name of the heartbeat.
    - **period** (integer) - The check interval in seconds.
    - **grace** (integer) - The grace period in seconds before marking as down.
    - **call** (boolean) - Whether to make a call on failure.
    - **sms** (boolean) - Whether to send an SMS on failure.
    - **email** (boolean) - Whether to send an email on failure.
    - **push** (boolean) - Whether to send a push notification on failure.
    - **team_wait** (integer) - Wait time in seconds for team notifications.
    - **heartbeat_group_id** (string|null) - The ID of the heartbeat group.
    - **team_name** (string) - The name of the team associated with the heartbeat.
    - **sort_index** (null) - Sorting index for the heartbeat.
    - **paused_at** (string|null) - Timestamp when the heartbeat was paused.
    - **maintenance_from** (string) - Start time of maintenance window.
    - **maintenance_to** (string) - End time of maintenance window.
    - **maintenance_timezone** (string) - Timezone for the maintenance window.
    - **maintenance_days** (array of strings) - Days of the week for the maintenance window.
    - **created_at** (string) - Timestamp of creation.
    - **updated_at** (string) - Timestamp of last update.
    - **status** (string) - Current status of the heartbeat (e.g., "up", "down", "paused", "pending").

#### Response Example (200)
```json
{
  "data": {
    "id": "12345",
    "type": "heartbeat",
    "attributes": {
      "url": "https://uptime.betterstack.com/api/v1/heartbeat/abcd1234abcd1234abcd1234",
      "name": "Testing heartbeat",
      "period": 10800,
      "grace": 300,
      "call": false,
      "sms": false,
      "email": true,
      "push": true,
      "team_wait": 180,
      "heartbeat_group_id": null,
      "team_name": "Test team",
      "sort_index": null,
      "paused_at": null,
      "maintenance_from": "01:02:00",
      "maintenance_to": "03:04:00",
      "maintenance_timezone": "Amsterdam",
      "maintenance_days": ["mon", "tue", "wed", "thu", "fri", "sat", "sun"],
      "created_at": "2020-05-05T11:41:49.327Z",
      "updated_at": "2020-12-10T15:00:15.089Z",
      "status": "up"
    }
  }
}
```

#### Error Responses
Refer to the general API documentation for common error responses.
```

--------------------------------

### Playwright: Retry Failing Tests

Source: https://betterstack.com/docs/uptime/playwright-monitor

This Playwright test configuration enables automatic retries for failing tests, up to a specified number of times. The `test.describe.configure({ retries: 2 })` line instructs Playwright to rerun any test that fails up to two additional times. This helps mitigate issues caused by intermittent failures or flaky tests. An example test is included that adds randomness to ensure retries can be observed.

```javascript
[label Retrying failing tests up to 2 times]
import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test';

// Retries failing tests up to 2 times
test.describe.configure({ retries: 2 });

test('has title', async ({ page }) => {
  await page.goto('https://betterstack.com/')
  await expect(page).toHaveTitle(/Better Stack/)

  // Add some randomness to the results to test retry
  // A single test succeeds only in 50% of cases
  await expect(Math.random() > 0.5).toBeTruthy()
});
```
